original: Antioch surrenders to the Muslim forces under Rashidun Caliphate after the Battle of Iron bridge
John VI of Greece begins his reign as Catholic Pope
Alberic II of Spoleto elevates nominates Marinus II as Cathloic Pope who was also erroneously called Martin III (Martinus III)
The Eighth Crusade and siege of Tunis ended by agreement between Charles I of Sicily (brother to King Louis IX of France, who had died months earlier) and the sultan of Tunis.

Battle of Rio Salado (or Tarifa): King Afonso IV of Portugal and King Alfonso XI of Castile defeat Sultan Abu al-Hasan 'Ali of Morocco and Yusuf I of Granada in the last Marinid invasion of the Iberian Peninsula
Queen Isabella of Spain bans violence against Indians in the Americas
Gustavus Adolphus becomes King of Sweden at 17 (1611-32)
Germany signs French/English/Spanish/Dutch/Brandenburgs peace treaty ending the Nine Years' War
Great Britain declares war on Spain: War of Jenkins' Ear [NS=Oct 19]
St. Paul's Chapel in New York is consecrated
First Methodist church in North American colonies initiated (Wesley Chapel, NYC)
First public railway in the Russian Empire opens - the Tsarskoye Selo Railway, running 27 km from Saint Petersburg to Pavlovsk
Alfred de Musset's "Bettine" premieres in Paris
Helena, Montana's capital, founded
Treaty of Vienna signed to end the Second Schleswig War - Denmark loses control of Schleswig to Prussia, and of Holstein to Austria
John Menard of Louisiana is 1st African American elected to US Congress
Philadelphia Athletics beat Chicago for 1st National Association baseball pennant
Austria-Hungary, Germany and Romania sign military treaty
Great Britain and Germany agree boundaries in East Africa

US Senate approves repealing Sherman Silver Purchase Act of 1890

Domenico Melegatti obtains a patent for a procedure to be applied in industrial production of pandoro (a traditional Italian sweet yeast bread).
Battle of Ladysmith: Boers defeat the British, leading to the Siege of Ladysmith in Natal
The Second Boer War: Battle at Bakenlaagte - British Army Lieutenant-Colonel Benson's rear guard is overwhelmed by Boer General Botha's troops
In violation of their promise to evacuate Manchuria, the Russian reoccupy Mukden and reinforce their troops in Manchuria
Allied offensive at Ypres (Belgium), aka 'Wipers' during WWI begins
Slovakia asks for creation of Czechoslovakian state
WWI: Turkey signs an armistice with the Allies, agreeing to end hostilities at noon on 31 October
Baseball league presidents call for abolishment of spitball

The Stuttgart Cable Car is constructed in Germany

20 die & 6,000 made ill by smog in Donora, Pennsylvania

1st use of 24-sec shot clock in pro basketball (Rochester vs Boston)

Businessman and horse breeder William Woodward, Jr. is shot dead by his wife Ann Cromwell after she mistook him for an intruder
Imtiaz Ahmed scores 209 v NZ, the record for a no 8 batsman
Dodgers sell Ebbets Field to a real estate group. They agree to stay until 1959, with an option to stay until 1961

Serious riots in the Catholic Ardoyne area of Belfast begin. lasting for three nights
Loyalist paramilitaries carry out a raid on Royal Ulster Constabulary station in County Derry, and steal 4 British Army Sterling sub-machine Guns

The Northern Ireland Office issues a discussion document 'The Future of Northern Ireland'; the paper states Britain's commitment to the union as long as the majority of people wish to remain part of the United Kingdom
Worst US rail accident in 14 years; 45 die in Chicago, llinois

Indonesian medical tragedy as 157 children have now died from kidney injuries this year due to contaminated medicines according to authorities
